subject: [PATCH 2/3] nfsd: fix UAF in cpntf statelist drain

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS server's statelist drain function, nfs4_free_cpntf_statelist(). The bug occurs when a concurrent holder increments the reference count of a state while another thread is draining the list. The fix involves rewriting the drain to unconditionally revoke each entry before dropping its parent-owned reference, ensuring that all entries are properly removed from the list and freed in one pass.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown noted that the switch to list_for_each_entry_safe() is unnecessary and prefers using a while loop instead, citing clarity in list emptiness
reviews.raw_body:
The switch from while() to list_for_each_entry_safe() isn't required for
this change, and isn't justified by the commit message.

I prefer while() is it makes it abundantly clear that the list will be
emptied.
I personally prefer

  while ((cps = list_first_entry_or_null(&stid->sc_cp_list,
				       struct nfs4_cpntf_state,
				       cp_list)) != NULL) {

but I understand that might not be everyone's favourite.

Apart from the comment, the code changes looks correct.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS server's cpntf statelist drain function. The bug occurs when a concurrent holder increments the reference count of a state, causing the drain to re-decrement the same entry on subsequent iterations and burn the original reference held by the concurrent holder. To fix this, the patch rewrites the drain function to unconditionally revoke each entry before dropping the parent's reference, ensuring that the drain terminates in one pass per entry regardless of the reference count.
